title: Pinning ontology-grounded post-extraction correction (arXiv:2605.29168 + neighbors), the no-derivation distinguishing sentence, the extractor-as-interchangeable-component framing, and the final ACL-KE venue note
layman_summary: Web-only research that nails down one more group of related papers and writes a few precise sentences so reviewers see the paper's contribution as clearly distinct from existing knowledge-graph-correction and relation-extraction work.
summary: Pure-web ($0, no code, cpu_light) research that closes the LAST iter-9 ACL-Knowledge-Extraction (ACL-KE) reviewer NOVELTY MINOR for the closure-certificate paper and preempts the obvious KE reaction to iter-10's supervised, precision-preserving extractor. WS1 pins, with web-verified @misc/@inproceedings BibTeX, method TYPE, and verbatim quotes, three ontology-grounded post-extraction-correction / KG-construction foils, each of which REPAIRS/COMMITS a labeling against EXTERNAL ontological structure and does NOT abstain: PRIMARY Loconte2026PostExtractionCorrection [arXiv:2605.29168, 'Better Later Than Sooner', Loconte/Hospedales/Cornelio, Edinburgh+Samsung AI Cambridge, submitted 27 May 2026, cs.AI] -- the OAK+MEND method that does 'targeted LLM-based correction of ontology violations' by 'deferring corrections to a post-extraction stage', detecting domain-range violations with symbolic rules derived from the ontology and correcting them via LLM calls (ontology-consistency up to 98.4%/96.8%); NEIGHBOR-1 Chepurova2026Wikontic [arXiv:2512.00590, Wikontic, v1 Nov 2025 / v2 Jan 2026, cs.CL] -- a multi-stage pipeline 'enforcing Wikidata-based type and relation constraints' that 'enforces schema and ontology constraints on entity types and domain-range'; NEIGHBOR-2 Feng2024OntologyGrounded [arXiv:2412.20942, CONFIRMED venue CEUR-WS Vol-3841 / HI-AI@KDD 2024, CUHK] -- 'ground generation of KG with the authored ontology' aligned to the Wikidata schema. OntoKG [arXiv:2604.02618] was inspected and REJECTED as the neighbor (schema-design over a Wikidata dump, not post-extraction correction of text-extracted facts). WS1 also writes the single drop-in distinguishing sentence (no-derivation certificate = per-query, gold-free abstention from deductive closure over the DOCUMENT-INTERNAL graph vs ontology-grounded post-extraction correction = repairs/commits labels against EXTERNAL structure; gold-free/training-free/no-external-KB delta preserved), a THIRD external-structure foil distinct from Qin2026's external-KB RAG and Sansford2024's NLI-vs-context. WS2 writes the crisp framing that iter-10's precision-preserving supervised extractor is standard, INTERCHANGEABLE RE plumbing (the gold-read ceiling 1.0/1.0/1.0 localizes all net-utility headroom to extraction recall, not the certificate), NOT the contribution and NOT a DEPTH/Li2024/Pi2025-style trained NO_RELATION refiner. WS3 gives the final ACL-KE-primary (NeSy/EMNLP fallback) venue note foregrounding atomic + multi-hop extraction P/R, leading with the RE NA problem, and positioning against all THREE external-structure foils. Deliverables: 3 BibTeX entries (new) + reused-key list, the distinguishing sentence, the extractor framing, the venue note, and a 1-row method-delta-table addition. Feeds the iter-10 Related-Work, Introduction, and method-section framing.
answer:
This artifact closes the FINAL iter-9 ACL-Knowledge-Extraction (ACL-KE) reviewer NOVELTY MINOR for the closure-certificate paper and inoculates iter-10's central methodological move -- a supervised, precision-preserving extractor -- against the most predictable KE objection. It is pure $0 web research; all four candidate papers were verified by direct arXiv abstract fetch + PDF fetch_grep, and it reuses the dependency art_mxFG0bGhTe2- \cite keys verbatim (Yang2025DEPTH, Li2024RelClassifier, Pi2025RelPrior, Qin2026PremiseVerification, Sansford2024GraphEval, plus inherited iter-8 keys).

WORKSTREAM 1 -- PIN THE ONTOLOGY-GROUNDED POST-EXTRACTION-CORRECTION FOIL + THE DISTINGUISHING SENTENCE. The reviewer asked for one sentence distinguishing the per-query, gold-free no-derivation certificate from ontology-grounded POST-EXTRACTION structural correction. The PRIMARY foil is verified: 'Better Later Than Sooner: Neuro-Symbolic Knowledge Graph Construction via Ontology-grounded Post-extraction Correction' by Lorenzo Loconte, Timothy Hospedales, Cristina Cornelio (University of Edinburgh / Samsung AI Center, Cambridge), submitted 27 May 2026, cs.AI/cs.LG, no peer-reviewed venue -> arXiv preprint [1,2]. The method (named OAK+MEND in the body) is exactly the foil the hypothesis names: it 'propose[s] a neuro-symbolic framework for ontology-grounded KG construction combining open-domain extraction, embedding-based canonicalization of types and predicates, and targeted LLM-based correction of ontology violations', and 'By deferring corrections to a post-extraction stage, our method avoids repeated LLM calls' [1,2]. The body makes the external-structure, commit-not-abstain character unambiguous: 'Symbolic rules derived from the ontology detect domain-range violations (both for triples and qualifiers). Detected inconsistencies are corrected through LLM calls, producing a consistent KG'; 'in Section 4 we develop an approach to systematically correct violations of ontology constraints'; it reports 'ontology consistency rates for triples and qualifiers of up to 98.4% and 96.8% respectively' [2]. So it operates AFTER extraction, against an EXTERNAL ontology, and COMMITS a corrected labeling -- it never abstains. NEIGHBOR-1 is Wikontic (arXiv:2512.00590; Alla Chepurova, Aydar Bulatov, Mikhail Burtsev, Yuri Kuratov; v1 29 Nov 2025, v2 29 Jan 2026, cs.CL), a 'multi-stage pipeline that constructs KGs from open-domain texts by extracting candidate triplets with qualifiers, enforcing Wikidata-based type and relation constraints, and normalizing entities', which '(2) enforces schema and ontology constraints on entity types and domain-range' and 'integrate[s] a large-scale ontology from Wikidata directly into the information extraction pipeline' [3,4]. NEIGHBOR-2 is Feng et al. (arXiv:2412.20942; Xiaohan Feng, Xixin Wu, Helen Meng, CUHK), chosen over OntoKG because it is the on-title 'under Wikidata schema' match, explicitly grounds/commits extracted relations against the external Wikidata ontology -- 'we ground generation of KG with the authored ontology based on extracted relations' and 'use the resulting ontology to ground the transformation of CQ-answer pairs into a structured KG' [5,6] -- AND carries a CONFIRMED peer-reviewed-workshop venue: CEUR Workshop Proceedings 3841 (2024) 117-135, HI-AI@KDD 2024 (Human-Interpretable AI Workshop at KDD 2024, Barcelona) [5]. OntoKG (arXiv:2604.02618, 'Ontology-Oriented Knowledge Graph Construction with Intrinsic-Relational Routing', submitted 3 Apr 2026) was inspected and REJECTED as the neighbor: it is a schema-DESIGN paper -- 'intrinsic-relational routing ... classifies every property as either intrinsic or relational and routes it to the corresponding schema module' over a 34.6M-entity Wikidata-dump core into 94 modules -- not post-extraction CORRECTION of text-extracted facts against an external ontology [7]. Only two neighbors are kept (Wikontic + Feng), per the objective. The single drop-in distinguishing sentence (full LaTeX in distinguishing_sentence) names ontology-grounded post-extraction correction with the three new keys, says it 'repairs and commits' an extracted labeling against EXTERNAL structure, and contrasts our certificate as a 'per-query, gold-free abstention' from deductive closure over the DOCUMENT-INTERNAL relation graph, preserving the gold-free / training-free / no-external-KB delta. It is a THIRD external-structure foil, distinct from Qin2026PremiseVerification (external-KB RAG) and Sansford2024GraphEval (NLI-vs-provided-context), and does not duplicate those sentences.

WORKSTREAM 2 -- PREEMPT THE SUPERVISED-EXTRACTOR KE REACTION (extractor = interchangeable component). iter-10 introduces a SUPERVISED precision-preserving extractor as the deployable net-utility route; a KE reviewer will ask whether this is just a DEPTH/Li2024/Pi2025-style trained NO_RELATION refiner. The framing (full LaTeX in workstream_2.extractor_as_component_latex) states: (i) the precision-preserving extractor -- a supervised span tagger, a distilled extraction model, or a constrained-decoding reader, all interchangeable and all standard structured-extraction plumbing [8] -- slots in freely because the gold-read ceiling (1.0/1.0/1.0 on the natural-prose metrics) localizes the ENTIRE net-utility headroom to extraction recall, not to the certificate; (ii) the contribution is therefore neither the extractor nor a better NO_RELATION refiner in the sense of Yang2025DEPTH/Li2024RelClassifier/Pi2025RelPrior -- whose trained refiners ARE their contribution -- but the compositional false-premise diagnostic plus the gold-free, training-free, no-external-KB structural certificate; (iii) that certificate runs UNCHANGED downstream of any such extractor and catches the multi-hop, document-internal absent-relation premises that neither the confidence family nor a same-model or stronger cross-family query-side verifier filters. This is consistent with the hypothesis HONESTY COMMITMENT: the certificate MECHANISM is the inherited NeSy deduce-then-abstain premise; novelty = the diagnostic + the catching win + the recall-precision frontier; the supervised extractor is NOT claimed as novel. The constrained-decoding / supervised-tagging interchange is well documented in the structured-IE literature [8] and is cited only to phrase the 'standard plumbing' claim defensibly, not as a load-bearing baseline.

WORKSTREAM 3 -- FINAL ACL-KE-PRIMARY VENUE NOTE. The ~160-word note (full LaTeX in workstream_3.venue_note_latex) reaffirms ACL-KE as PRIMARY with NeSy/EMNLP fallback; leads the framing with the RE NA problem (Yang2025DEPTH/Li2024RelClassifier/Pi2025RelPrior) so the contribution reads as a familiar KE capability, with the relation-algebra/closure machinery kept as the deductive engine behind NA detection rather than the headline; foregrounds KE metrics -- atomic fact-extraction precision/recall directly from the document, multi-hop fact-extraction and deduction accuracy, and confident-wrong rate on absent pairs at matched coverage; and positions against ALL THREE external-structure foils that now jointly motivate the document-internal, gold-free, training-free stance: external-KB RAG premise checks (Qin2026PremiseVerification), NLI-vs-provided-context triple checks (Sansford2024GraphEval), and ontology-grounded post-extraction correction (Loconte2026PostExtractionCorrection). This is fully consistent with the dependency's venue recommendation and does not contradict it.

The method-delta-table (workstream_1.method_delta_row) adds one row for 'Ontology-grounded post-extraction correction' on the dependency's seven axes: external KB/structure = YES (external ontology / Wikidata schema); training = mixed (LLM-based correction + embedding canonicalization); trained model = embeddings + LLM corrector; granularity = triple/qualifier labeling; logic = rule-based domain-range violation detection then correction toward the external ontology; absent-relation handling = NONE / commits a corrected label, does not abstain; operates on = open-domain extracted KG vs external ontology. That keeps the gold-free + training-free + no-external-KB + compositional deductive 'no derivation path -> abstain' cell empty -- DELTA-2 -- now against a third, ontology-based external-structure family.

pinned_papers.role: PRIMARY iter-10 foil -- ontology-grounded POST-EXTRACTION correction that REPAIRS/COMMITS extracted triples against an EXTERNAL ontology (the exact foil the reviewer named)
pinned_papers.title: Better Later Than Sooner: Neuro-Symbolic Knowledge Graph Construction via Ontology-grounded Post-extraction Correction
pinned_papers.authors: Lorenzo Loconte, Timothy Hospedales, Cristina Cornelio (University of Edinburgh, UK; Samsung AI Center, Cambridge, UK)
pinned_papers.venue: arXiv preprint 2605.29168 (cs.AI; cross-list cs.LG); submitted 27 May 2026 (v1, 2026-05-27 23:09:10 UTC); no peer-reviewed venue listed -> arXiv preprint, venue UNCONFIRMED. License CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
pinned_papers.method_name: OAK+MEND (open-domain extraction + canonicalization, then ontology-rule violation detection + LLM-based correction)
pinned_papers.method_type: Neuro-symbolic post-hoc CORRECTION against an EXTERNAL ontology: open-domain LLM KG extraction -> embedding-based canonicalization of types/predicates to the ontology -> symbolic rules detect domain-range (ontology-constraint) violations -> targeted LLM calls correct them, producing a consistent KG. NOT gold-free (uses an external ontology), NOT abstention (COMMITS a corrected labeling).
pinned_papers.requires_external_KB: YES -- an external ontology/schema (domain-range type constraints); canonicalizes extracted types/predicates to those allowed by the ontology.
pinned_papers.commits_vs_abstains: COMMITS -- it repairs/corrects extracted triples toward ontology consistency (up to 98.4%/96.8% consistency for triples/qualifiers); it never issues a no-derivation abstention.
verbatim_quotes: Yet these pipelines typically use LLMs for KG extraction, which can introduce consistency issues, where extracted facts may violate commonsense ontology constraints. (Abstract)

We propose a neuro-symbolic framework for ontology-grounded KG construction combining open-domain extraction, embedding-based canonicalization of types and predicates, and targeted LLM-based correction of ontology violations. (Abstract)
By deferring corrections to a post-extraction stage, our method avoids repeated LLM calls, substantially reducing token usage while improving KG consistency. (Abstract)

Symbolic rules derived from the ontology detect domain-range violations (both for triples and qualifiers). Detected inconsistencies are corrected through LLM calls, producing a consistent KG (Figure 2, OAK+MEND overview)
in Section 4 we develop an approach to systematically correct violations of ontology constraints. (Sec. 2/3)
achieving ontology consistency rates for triples and qualifiers of up to 98.4% and 96.8% respectively. (Sec. Contributions)
pinned_papers.why_it_is_the_foil: It is the paper the iter-9 reviewer explicitly asked us to distinguish from: ontology-grounded POST-EXTRACTION correction. It operates after extraction, uses an EXTERNAL ontology/schema, and COMMITS a corrected labeling -- the precise contrast to our per-query, gold-free, document-internal no-derivation abstention. It joins Qin2026 (external-KB RAG) and Sansford2024 (NLI-vs-context) as a THIRD external-structure foil.

pinned_papers.role: NEIGHBOR-1 -- Wikidata-aligned, ontology-aware KG construction that ENFORCES external schema/ontology constraints on extracted triples
pinned_papers.title: Wikontic: Constructing Wikidata-Aligned, Ontology-Aware Knowledge Graphs with Large Language Models
pinned_papers.authors: Alla Chepurova, Aydar Bulatov, Mikhail Burtsev, Yuri Kuratov (Cognitive AI Systems Lab / AIRI, Moscow)
pinned_papers.venue: arXiv preprint 2512.00590 (cs.CL; cross-list cs.AI, cs.LG); v1 29 Nov 2025, v2 29 Jan 2026 (this version); no peer-reviewed venue listed -> venue UNCONFIRMED.
pinned_papers.method_type: Multi-stage construction with EXTERNAL Wikidata schema-constraint enforcement: extract candidate triplets with qualifiers -> ontology-aware triplet refinement enforcing schema/domain-range constraints -> alias-aware entity normalization/deduplication -> Wikidata-aligned, ontology-consistent KG. Commits an aligned KG (no abstention).
pinned_papers.requires_external_KB: YES -- a large-scale ontology derived from Wikidata integrated directly into the extraction pipeline; enforces Wikidata-based type and relation constraints.
pinned_papers.commits_vs_abstains: COMMITS -- produces a Wikidata-aligned, ontology-consistent KG; refines/normalizes rather than abstaining.
verbatim_quotes: a multi-stage pipeline that constructs KGs from open-domain texts by extracting candidate triplets with qualifiers, enforcing Wikidata-based type and relation constraints, and normalizing entities to reduce duplication. (Abstract)
we integrate a large-scale ontology from Wikidata directly into the information extraction pipeline. (Sec. Introduction)
(2) enforces schema and ontology constraints on entity types and domain-range, and (3) performs alias-aware entity normalization and deduplication (Contributions list)
pinned_papers.why_it_is_the_foil: Closest ontology-aware KG-construction neighbor: it iteratively aligns/refines each triple against an EXTERNAL Wikidata ontology/schema and commits the result -- contrasting our document-internal, gold-free, no-schema-alignment abstention.

pinned_papers.role: NEIGHBOR-2 (venue-confirmed) -- ontology-grounded KG construction that GROUNDS/COMMITS extracted relations against the EXTERNAL Wikidata schema
pinned_papers.title: Ontology-grounded Automatic Knowledge Graph Construction by LLM under Wikidata schema
pinned_papers.authors: Xiaohan Feng, Xixin Wu, Helen Meng (Chinese University of Hong Kong)
pinned_papers.venue: HI-AI@KDD 2024 (Human-Interpretable AI Workshop at KDD 2024, 26 Aug 2024, Barcelona); CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-3841 (2024), pp. 117-135. CONFIRMED via arXiv journal-ref + Comments fields. Also arXiv:2412.20942 (cs.AI; cross-list cs.IR), v1 30 Dec 2024.
pinned_papers.method_type: Ontology-grounded construction: author an ontology via Competency Questions, match candidate properties to Wikidata (extending the schema as needed), then GROUND KG generation in the authored/Wikidata-aligned ontology. Commits an ontology-grounded, Wikidata-interoperable KG.
pinned_papers.requires_external_KB: YES -- the external Wikidata schema/ontology; candidate properties matched against Wikidata definitions.
pinned_papers.commits_vs_abstains: COMMITS -- grounds the transformation of CQ-answer pairs into a structured KG against the authored/Wikidata ontology; no abstention.
verbatim_quotes: To ensure consistency and interpretability in the resulting KG, we ground generation of KG with the authored ontology based on extracted relations. (Abstract)

matching candidate properties against those defined in Wikidata and extending the schema as needed. Finally, we use the resulting ontology to ground the transformation of CQ-answer pairs into a structured KG. (Sec. Introduction)
Journal reference: CEUR Workshop Proceedings 3841 (2024) 117-135 ; Presented at HI-AI@KDD, Human-Interpretable AI Workshop at the KDD 2024 (arXiv metadata)
pinned_papers.why_it_is_the_foil: On-title 'under Wikidata schema' match with a CONFIRMED workshop venue (CEUR-WS Vol-3841); grounds/commits extracted relations against the EXTERNAL Wikidata ontology -- the same repairs-toward-external-structure character as the primary, providing a peer-reviewed anchor for the cluster.

rejected_neighbor.title: OntoKG: Ontology-Oriented Knowledge Graph Construction with Intrinsic-Relational Routing (Yitao Li, Zhanlin Liu, Anuranjan Pandey, Muni Srikanth; submitted 3 Apr 2026, cs.AI)
rejected_neighbor.reason: Inspected and REJECTED as the neighbor: it is a SCHEMA-DESIGN paper -- 'intrinsic-relational routing ... classifies every property as either intrinsic or relational and routes it to the corresponding schema module' over a 34.6M-entity Wikidata-dump core into 94 modules -- NOT post-extraction CORRECTION of text-extracted facts against an external ontology. Weaker fit than Feng2024; objective caps neighbors at 2, so it is not added (no padding).
rejected_neighbor.verbatim_quote: The core mechanism is intrinsic-relational routing, which classifies every property as either intrinsic or relational and routes it to the corresponding schema module. (Abstract)

method_delta_row.external KB / retrieval: YES (external ontology / Wikidata schema; domain-range type constraints)
method_delta_row.requires training / fine-tuning: Mixed (LLM-based correction + embedding-based canonicalization; no large-scale RE fine-tuning, but LLM calls + embedding models)
method_delta_row.requires a trained model (NLI/classifier: Embedding model for type/predicate canonicalization + LLM corrector
method_delta_row.logic: rule-based domain-range violation detection, then correction TOWARD the external ontology
method_delta_row.absent-relation handling: NONE -- commits a corrected label, does not abstain
method_delta_row.operates on: open-domain extracted KG vs external ontology / Wikidata schema

distinguishing_sentence: Distinct from ontology-grounded post-extraction correction, which \emph{repairs and commits} an extracted labeling against an \emph{external} ontology or schema \citep{Loconte2026PostExtractionCorrection,Chepurova2026Wikontic,Feng2024OntologyGrounded}, our no-derivation certificate issues a \emph{per-query, gold-free abstention} obtained from deductive closure over the \emph{document-internal} relation graph alone---requiring no external KB, no schema alignment, and no training---so it certifies the \emph{absence of a derivation path} rather than correcting a labeling toward external structure.

workstream_2.risk: A KE reviewer sees iter-10's precision-preserving SUPERVISED extractor and conflates it with the DEPTH/Li2024/Pi2025 trained NO_RELATION-refiner line, then asks 'isn't your contribution just another trained RE refiner?'.
workstream_2.extractor_as_component_latex: The precision-preserving extractor of our deployable variant is standard, interchangeable relation-extraction plumbing---a supervised span tagger, a distilled extraction model, or a constrained-decoding reader all serve---since the gold-read ceiling ($1.0/1.0/1.0$ on the natural-prose metrics) localizes the entire net-utility headroom to extraction \emph{recall}, not to the certificate. The contribution is therefore neither the extractor nor a better \textsc{no\_relation} refiner in the sense of \citet{Yang2025DEPTH,Li2024RelClassifier,Pi2025RelPrior}---whose trained refiners \emph{are} the contribution---but the compositional false-premise diagnostic together with the gold-free, training-free, no-external-KB structural certificate, which runs unchanged downstream of any such extractor and catches the multi-hop, document-internal absent-relation premises that neither the confidence family nor a same-model or stronger cross-family query-side verifier filters.
workstream_2.honesty_consistency: Consistent with HONESTY COMMITMENT (3): the certificate MECHANISM is the inherited NeSy deduce-then-abstain premise; novelty = the compositional false-premise DIAGNOSTIC + the catching win + the recall-precision frontier; the supervised extractor is NOT claimed as novel. The supervised-tagging / constrained-decoding interchange is documented in the structured-IE literature [8] and is cited only to phrase the 'standard plumbing' claim defensibly, NOT as a load-bearing baseline.

workstream_3.venue_note_latex: \textbf{Venue.} We target the ACL Knowledge-Extraction (KE) track as the primary venue, with NeSy / EMNLP as fallback. The framing leads with the relation-extraction \textsc{no\_relation}/NA problem---LLM extractors over-predicting relations on entity pairs that bear none \citep{Yang2025DEPTH,Li2024RelClassifier,Pi2025RelPrior}---so the contribution reads as a familiar KE capability, with the relation-algebra / composition-closure machinery kept as the deductive engine behind NA detection rather than the headline. KE metrics are foregrounded: atomic fact-extraction precision/recall directly from the document, multi-hop fact-extraction and deduction accuracy, and confident-wrong rate on absent pairs at matched coverage. Positioning is against three distinct external-structure foils that now jointly motivate our document-internal, gold-free, training-free stance: external-KB RAG premise checks \citep{Qin2026PremiseVerification}, NLI-vs-provided-context triple checks \citep{Sansford2024GraphEval}, and ontology-grounded post-extraction correction \citep{Loconte2026PostExtractionCorrection}. The NeSy/EMNLP fallback leads instead with deduce-then-abstain soundness and risk-coverage.
workstream_3.consistency_note: Consistent with the dependency art_mxFG0bGhTe2- recommendation (ACL-KE primary, NeSy/EMNLP fallback); does not contradict it. Adds only the third external-structure foil (external-ontology post-extraction correction) to the joint motivation.

sources.title: Better Later Than Sooner: Neuro-Symbolic Knowledge Graph Construction via Ontology-grounded Post-extraction Correction (Loconte, Hospedales, Cornelio)
sources.summary: arXiv abstract page (v1, submitted 27 May 2026 23:09 UTC; cs.AI cross-list cs.LG; no peer-reviewed venue -> arXiv preprint). Authors Lorenzo Loconte, Timothy Hospedales, Cristina Cornelio (Univ. of Edinburgh / Samsung AI Center, Cambridge). Abstract verbatim anchors: 'extracted facts may violate commonsense ontology constraints'; 'a neuro-symbolic framework for ontology-grounded KG construction combining open-domain extraction, embedding-based canonicalization of types and predicates, and targeted LLM-based correction of ontology violations'; 'By deferring corrections to a post-extraction stage, our method avoids repeated LLM calls'. PRIMARY foil: repairs/commits a labeling against an EXTERNAL ontology after extraction.

Detected inconsistencies are corrected through LLM calls, producing a consistent KG' (Figure 2); 'in Section 4 we develop an approach to systematically correct violations of ontology constraints'; 'We map (or canonicalize) the open-domain types and predicates extracted in Step 1 to types and predicates allowed by the ontology'; 'achieving ontology consistency rates for triples and qualifiers of up to 98.4% and 96.8% respectively'. Confirms post-extraction, EXTERNAL-ontology, COMMIT-not-abstain character.

sources.title: Wikontic PDF (grepped) -- integrates Wikidata ontology into IE pipeline, enforces schema/domain-range constraints
sources.summary: fetch_grep extraction. VERBATIM: 'we integrate a large-scale ontology from Wikidata directly into the information extraction pipeline'; contributions '(2) enforces schema and ontology constraints on entity types and domain-range, and (3) performs alias-aware entity normalization and deduplication'; six components incl. 'ontology-aware triplet refinement enforcing schema constraints'. Confirms external-ontology enforcement + commit (no abstention).

sources.title: Ontology-grounded Automatic Knowledge Graph Construction by LLM under Wikidata schema (Feng, Wu, Meng) -- CEUR-WS Vol-3841 / HI-AI@KDD 2024
sources.summary: arXiv abstract page (v1 30 Dec 2024; cs.AI cross-list cs.IR). Authors Xiaohan Feng, Xixin Wu, Helen Meng (CUHK). CONFIRMED venue: journal-ref 'CEUR Workshop Proceedings 3841 (2024) 117-135'; Comments 'Presented at HI-AI@KDD, Human-Interpretable AI Workshop at the KDD 2024, 26 Aug 2024, Barcelona'. Verbatim: 'we ground generation of KG with the authored ontology based on extracted relations'; 'interoperable with Wikidata semantics'. NEIGHBOR-2 (venue-confirmed).

sources.title: 2412.20942 PDF (grepped) -- author ontology via Competency Questions, match to Wikidata, ground KG generation
sources.summary: fetch_grep extraction. VERBATIM: 'An ontology is authored by generating Competency Questions (CQ) ... extracting relations from CQs, and attempt to replace equivalent relations by their counterpart in Wikidata. To ensure consistency and interpretability in the resulting KG, we ground generation of KG with the authored ontology based on extracted relations'; 'matching candidate properties against those defined in Wikidata and extending the schema as needed. Finally, we use the resulting ontology to ground the transformation of CQ-answer pairs into a structured KG'. Confirms ontology-grounding/commit against external Wikidata schema.

sources.title: OntoKG: Ontology-Oriented Knowledge Graph Construction with Intrinsic-Relational Routing (Li et al.) -- inspected, REJECTED as neighbor
sources.summary: arXiv abstract page (v1 3 Apr 2026, cs.AI). Authors Yitao Li, Zhanlin Liu, Anuranjan Pandey, Muni Srikanth. Verbatim: 'The core mechanism is intrinsic-relational routing, which classifies every property as either intrinsic or relational and routes it to the corresponding schema module'; instantiated on the Jan-2026 Wikidata dump (34.6M-entity core, 94 modules, 34.0M nodes / 61.2M edges). REJECTED as the neighbor: this is schema-DESIGN/property-routing over a Wikidata dump, not post-extraction CORRECTION of text-extracted facts against an external ontology. Documented for transparency; not cited (objective caps neighbors at 2).

sources.title: Grammar-Constrained Decoding for Structured NLP Tasks without Finetuning (representative; WS2 phrasing only)
sources.summary: Representative source for the WS2 'standard, interchangeable RE plumbing' phrasing: casts structured NLP/extraction tasks as constrained decoding from an LLM where only outputs conforming to a task-specific grammar are allowed, alongside supervised taggers and distilled extraction models as standard structured-IE approaches. Cited ONLY to phrase the interchangeable-component claim defensibly; NOT a load-bearing baseline for the paper. (Search also surfaced grammar-constrained decoding for IE at PMC11747381 and the SIGDIAL-2024 dialogue-ontology RE work, corroborating that constrained decoding is a standard structured-extraction technique.)

follow_up_questions: Will arXiv:2605.29168 (OAK+MEND) and Wikontic (arXiv:2512.00590) land at a peer-reviewed venue by our camera-ready, so the @misc preprint cites can be upgraded to @inproceedings/@article (Feng2024 already has a confirmed CEUR-WS Vol-3841 / HI-AI@KDD 2024 venue)?

Should we add an ontology-grounded post-extraction-correction baseline (e.g., an OAK+MEND-style domain-range corrector over a small external schema) to empirically harden the 'no-external-KB / document-internal' delta, or does that exceed the $0 / commodity-hardware / reproducibility constraints and risk muddying the gold-free claim?

Is there any published dataset of compositional/multi-hop FALSE relational premises (as opposed to the single-edge KG-FPQ / SciERC / DocRED NA pairs) we could cite or adapt to further strengthen DELTA-1, or is our document-internal closure corpus the first such evaluation?

Given iter-10's supervised precision-preserving extractor, should the paper report a small ablation swapping the extractor (supervised tagger vs constrained-decoding reader) to empirically demonstrate the 'interchangeable plumbing' claim and that the certificate runs unchanged downstream?
